questions.question:
Assertion (A): The common difference of the Arithmetic Progression $5, 2, -1, -4, ...$ is $-3$. 
Reason (R): The common difference $d$ of an Arithmetic Progression is calculated by the formula $d = a_{n} - a_{n-1}$ for any $n > 1$.

steps: Calculate the common difference using $d = a_2 - a_1$.
Confirm that the formula in Reason (R) is the definition of common difference and correctly explains the calculation in (A).
solution.derivation: d = a_2 - a_1 = 2 - 5 = -3. \text{ Formula } d = a_n - a_{n-1} \text{ is standard.}

options: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
